title: Sampacca × longifolia

It belongs to the genus Sampacca, which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Sampacca × longifolia is often referred to as the "long-leaved sampacca" due to its distinctive foliage. This plant is valued for its large, showy flowers and ability to thrive in warm, humid environments.

Traditionally, Sampacca × longifolia has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties.
